meta.description: E‑commerce storefront (Next.js), Laravel JSON API, Next.js admin. Bilingual FR/EN.

epics.name: Cart & checkout
epics.goal: Complete purchase with accurate totals and shipping.

epics.name: Payments
epics.goal: COD and CMI flows complete safely.

epics.name: Post‑purchase
epics.goal: Order confirmation and trust after sale.

epics.name: Public & admin API contracts
epics.goal: Stable JSON contracts for front and integrations.

userStories.title: View localized home and navigation
userStories.iWant: to land on the storefront in my locale with header, shop links, and footer
userStories.soThat: I can orient and reach the catalog quickly
acceptanceCriteria: Given locale fr or en, When I open the home URL, Then the main landmark is visible and the URL reflects the locale segment
Given a storefront route that is not the checkout shell, When the page renders, Then header and footer chrome are visible
Given the announcement marquee and sticky contact affordance, When I use primary navigation, Then they do not block completing primary navigation

userStories.title: Browse shop and category product listings
userStories.iWant: to browse categories and see published products
userStories.soThat: I can compare offers before PDP
acceptanceCriteria: Given the shop loads, When categories are loaded from the API, Then the storefront surfaces those categories
Given a category that has products, When I view its listing, Then only published products appear and pagination meta is present when applicable
Given an empty listing or empty category state, When it renders, Then the client does not throw errors

userStories.title: Open product detail from slug
userStories.iWant: to open a PDP by slug with price, stock signal, images, and add‑to‑cart
acceptanceCriteria: Given an unknown product slug, When GET /api/products/{slug} is requested, Then the API returns 404
Given a published product slug, When GET /api/products/{slug} is requested, Then the API returns 200 with a product payload
Given PDP with primary add-to-cart enabled, When automation targets ATC, Then the control exposes stable test id qa-pdp-atc-primary
Given API fields in_stock and stock_quantity, When PDP shows inventory messaging, Then copy matches those rules

userStories.title: Search and sort catalog
userStories.iWant: to search by name or slug and sort by featured or price
acceptanceCriteria: Given query parameters q, sort, and per_page within documented bounds, When GET /api/products is called, Then the API honors them
Given a catalog API failure such as 5xx, When the storefront catalog UI loads, Then the user sees an error path and not a silent empty success

userStories.title: Add line to cart from PDP
userStories.iWant: to add a SKU to the cart from the PDP primary button
acceptanceCriteria: Given I added a product from PDP, When the cart drawer or flow is open, Then a checkout CTA is available with test id qa-cart-checkout for automation
Given lines and quantities in the cart, When totals update on the client, Then quantity and line subtotal follow cart state rules

userStories.title: Guest checkout with address and shipping quote
userStories.iWant: to enter contact and address and receive shipping options
userStories.soThat: I know delivery cost before paying
acceptanceCriteria: Given a non-empty cart and a valid address payload, When POST /api/shipping/quote is called, Then returned methods are compatible with the cart and address
Given a shipping quote failure, When checkout displays the error, Then quote errors are visually distinct from address or field validation errors
Given an empty cart, When I try to proceed to checkout, Then checkout is blocked and a clear CTA returns me to the shop

userStories.title: Apply and validate promo code
userStories.iWant: to enter a promo code at checkout
userStories.soThat: I receive the advertised discount when valid
acceptanceCriteria: Given an invalid promo code at checkout, When I attempt to place the order, Then a localized error is shown and no order is created
Given a valid promo code, When checkout completes through the checkout API submit path, Then totals reflect the server-side promo adjustment

userStories.title: Place order with cash on delivery
userStories.iWant: to choose COD and place the order
acceptanceCriteria: Given COD is selected with a valid checkout payload, When POST /api/checkout succeeds, Then the response includes reference totals currency and lines
Given the checkout review step, When automation targets submit, Then the primary control exposes test id qa-checkout-submit
Given a successful checkout response, When the client finishes, Then confirmation is stored in session and the user is routed to thank-you with ref in the query string

userStories.title: Pay with CMI and return to storefront
userStories.iWant: to pay by card via CMI and land back on the storefront with clear status
acceptanceCriteria: Given CMI server notifications, When Laravel receives callbacks, Then the CMI callback route accepts them per implementation
Given the browser returns from CMI with success or failure query parameters, When the return URL is processed, Then status is clear and secrets are not exposed in the URL
Given CMI-related query parameters on checkout return, When the checkout client loads, Then submit state does not loop indefinitely

userStories.title: Thank you page shows order reference
userStories.iWant: to see confirmation copy and reference after purchase
userStories.soThat: I can trust the order was created
acceptanceCriteria: Given stored confirmation payload after purchase, When the thank-you view renders, Then FR/EN strings align with message bundles
Given an order reference, When GET /api/order-confirmation/{reference} is called, Then the payload is consistent with the placed order

userStories.title: Switch experience between French and English
userStories.iWant: the storefront strings and URLs to follow fr and en segments
acceptanceCriteria: Given Playwright projects fr and en, When E2E runs, Then baseURLs target …/fr/ and …/en/ respectively
Given critical checkout copy, When tests use locale datasets, Then placeholders remain exact-match testable per locale
Given SEO rules implemented for the storefront, When pages emit metadata, Then titles descriptions canonical and hreflang follow project SEO rules

userStories.title: Brand theme from API
userStories.iWant: colors and logo from brand endpoint
acceptanceCriteria: Given GET /api/brand returns a payload, When the storefront hydrates, Then StorefrontBrandProvider consumes it
Given SSR for first paint, When the page loads, Then the theme style block applies without a flash of the wrong brand colors

userStories.title: Products list contract
userStories.iWant: stable JSON for paginated products
userStories.soThat: front and tests do not break silently
acceptanceCriteria: Given GET /api/products with pagination, When the response returns 200, Then the body includes a data array and meta pagination fields
Given per_page outside the allowed range, When the request is processed, Then the API clamps per_page to 1–100
Given the QA/api REST Assured smoke for product list, When tests run, Then list returns 200 and JSON matches the expected shape

userStories.title: Checkout contract validation
userStories.iWant: checkout API to reject malformed payloads with 4xx
acceptanceCriteria: Given POST /api/checkout with missing lines invalid product_id or quantity zero, When validation runs, Then the API returns a structured validation error
Given concurrent stock changes during checkout, When OrderStockService applies rules, Then outcome matches documented stock behaviour

userStories.title: Contact message intake
userStories.iWant: to submit contact form to API
acceptanceCriteria: Given a valid POST /api/contact payload, When the request succeeds, Then the message is persisted and a success response is returned
Given rate limiting or spam controls exist, When they are documented, Then testers know expected behaviour for abuse scenarios

userStories.title: Admin authenticates
userStories.iWant: to log in to admin API with Sanctum
acceptanceCriteria: Given valid admin credentials, When POST /api/admin/login is called, Then a bearer token is issued
Given logout and me endpoints, When called with appropriate tokens and middleware, Then behaviour matches the configured middleware groups

userStories.title: Manage products and stock
userStories.iWant: CRUD on products, images, featured flag, and stock quantity
acceptanceCriteria: Given admin product routes, When the caller lacks auth:sanctum or admin.panel, Then access is denied; When authorized, Then CRUD behaves per route design
Given a stock update from admin, When the storefront reads the product, Then in_stock reflects the change within cache or freshness rules
Given an image upload request, When the file is processed, Then MIME type and size are validated

userStories.title: Operate orders
userStories.iWant: list, show, update orders and export where allowed
acceptanceCriteria: Given order listing with filters and pagination, When results return, Then they match agreed business rules
Given invoice or export routes, When the caller has admin.panel versus admin.full, Then access matches role middleware

userStories.title: Configure shipping methods and settings
userStories.iWant: to maintain shipping methods and site settings
acceptanceCriteria: Given shipping method CRUD where admin.full is required, When an unauthorized role calls the endpoint, Then the request fails; When authorized, Then CRUD succeeds per rules
Given settings upsert with keys documented in admin UI, When validation runs, Then invalid keys or values are rejected

userStories.title: CI pipeline proves API and critical E2E
userStories.iWant: CI to run REST Assured then Playwright on candidate builds
userStories.soThat: we do not ship obvious regressions
acceptanceCriteria: Given the GitHub Actions workflow at .github/workflows/qa.yml, When reviewed against the repo, Then jobs cover the quality gate, API contracts, and optional Playwright per workflow rules
Given a completed CI run, When artifacts are published, Then QA/playwright JUnit and QA/api Surefire outputs are retained where the workflow uploads them
Given repository security policy, When credentials are audited, Then secrets exist only in GitHub Actions secrets (or equivalent) not in Git

userStories.title: Traceability from story to automated test
userStories.iWant: each P0 story linked to tests or explicit manual only
userStories.soThat: audit and coverage gaps are visible
acceptanceCriteria: Given the traceability CSV path set in qa.config.json (paths.traceabilityCsv), When each Story_ID row is reviewed, Then Playwright refs API refs or Manual_Only_Notes satisfy the quality gate for P0
Given Playwright smoke specs under QA/playwright/tests/smoke, When compared to checkout behaviour in user stories, Then automation matches documented acceptance criteria

userStories.title: Staging environment parity
userStories.iWant: staging URLs for PLAYWRIGHT_ORIGIN and PLAYWRIGHT_API_BASE_URL
acceptanceCriteria: Given environment variables for Playwright and API base URL, When used locally versus CI, Then naming and semantics stay consistent
Given the default E2E product slug on staging, When tests run, Then that product exists and is in stock
